pi_items.source: Verified against Chinook database schema. Example query: SELECT GenreId, COUNT(*) as track_count FROM Track WHERE GenreId IS NOT NULL GROUP BY GenreId HAVING COUNT(*) > (SELECT AVG(...)) - returns 4 genres above average

https://github.com/lerocha/chinook-database - Official repository with documentation stating it is a sample database representing a digital media store

pi_items.verified_value: Digital media store with 11 tables: Album, Artist, Customer, Employee, Genre, Invoice, InvoiceLine, MediaType, Playlist, PlaylistTrack, Track. Media data based on real iTunes Library; customer and employee data is fictitious
pi_items.source: GitHub repository documentation and downloaded database inspection. Table count verified: 11 tables present in SQLite file downloaded from https://github.com/lerocha/chinook-database

Verified by downloading Chinook SQLite from official GitHub repository and running 'sqlite3 .schema Track'. Table has columns: TrackId, Name, AlbumId, MediaTypeId, GenreId, Composer, Milliseconds, Bytes, UnitPrice
Verified by downloading Chinook SQLite. Table has composite primary key (PlaylistId, TrackId) with foreign keys to Playlist and Track

Verified by downloading Chinook SQLite. Table has columns: InvoiceId, CustomerId, InvoiceDate, Total (plus billing address fields). Total 412 invoices
Verified by running 'sqlite3 SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Track' on downloaded Chinook SQLite database
Verified by running 'sqlite3 SELECT COUNT(*) FROM PlaylistTrack' on downloaded Chinook SQLite database

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the Chinook database, a real, publicly available sample database from https://github.com/lerocha/chinook-database representing a digital media store with 11 tables including Track (3503 rows), Genre (25 rows), Customer (59 rows), Invoice (412 rows), and PlaylistTrack (8715 rows). All three expected queries are feasible with the actual database schema: TracksNotInPlaylists would find tracks not in any playlist using NOT IN/NOT EXISTS against Track and PlaylistTrack tables; GenresAboveAvgTrackCount would identify genres with above-average track counts using AVG, GROUP BY, and HAVING; HighValueCustomers would find customers with above-average spending using SUM and AVG across Customer and Invoice tables. The database path /home/ga/chinook.odb is correctly specified and is created during environment setup by converting the SQLite version to LibreOffice Base ODB format.
